data.description:
## Why
The `/rebar-debug` skill (`examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md`) drives a
hypothesis-driven debugging protocol whose Phase 2 writes code (RED test + fix). It never
instructs the operator to isolate that work, so reproduction/experiments and the eventual
fix can land in the primary checkout on a stale branch — exactly the anti-pattern the
project's contributor rules forbid.

## What
Add a single setup directive at the very beginning of skill execution telling the agent to
switch to a fresh worktree based on `origin/main` before any other work.

## Scope
Docs/skill content only — `examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md`. No source or test
behavior changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md` contains an explicit instruction, placed
      at the start of skill execution, to switch to a fresh worktree based on `origin/main`.
- [ ] The instruction names the concrete mechanism (`git worktree add ... origin/main`).
- [ ] No other skill semantics change; the two-phase protocol is otherwise untouched.
- [ ] Change lands on `main` via Gerrit (LLM-Review +1 and Verified +1).
data.title: rebar-debug skill: switch to a fresh origin/main worktree at start of execution
